description:
## Problem Statement

Beadsmith renders Issue descriptions and comments as Markdown at a fixed 14px prose size. The fixed size is readable for some users but too dense or too large for others, and there is no Settings surface where a user can adapt the reading experience. Because the preference cannot be changed or remembered, the user must accept the built-in typography every time the app starts.

## Solution

Add a simple app-wide Settings page for Markdown typography. A Settings button at the bottom of the left sidebar replaces the main content with a focused settings page while keeping the sidebar visible. The page contains one numeric pixel input for Markdown font size, a live Markdown preview, inline validation, and a Reset action.

The setting is global across Workspaces and persists across app restarts in Beadsmith's supported Tauri-backed app-data storage. A valid input updates the preview immediately and applies to every rendered Markdown description and comment. Prose, headings, inline code, code blocks, lists, tables, and blockquotes scale proportionally from the chosen base size so the hierarchy remains coherent.

## User Stories

1. As a Beadsmith user, I want a Settings button in the left sidebar, so that preferences are easy to find from anywhere in the app.
2. As a Beadsmith user, I want Settings near the bottom of the sidebar, so that it is visually separate from Issue List Views and Issue Status choices.
3. As a Beadsmith user, I want opening Settings to keep the sidebar visible, so that the app retains its stable navigation frame.
4. As a Beadsmith user, I want Settings to replace the Issue Explorer content rather than open a modal, so that the page has focused space for current and future preferences.
5. As a Beadsmith user, I want to return from Settings to the Issue Explorer, so that changing a preference does not interrupt my work.
6. As a Beadsmith user, I want my active Issue List View to remain selected after visiting Settings, so that I return to the same work context.
7. As a Beadsmith user, I want my Issue Search query to remain intact after visiting Settings, so that I do not have to reconstruct a filtered view.
8. As a Beadsmith user, I want my selected Issue to remain selected after visiting Settings, so that I can compare the typography change against the same Issue.
9. As a Beadsmith user, I want to enter the Markdown font size directly as a number, so that I can choose an exact size without using a slider or predefined preset.
10. As a Beadsmith user, I want the input to use pixels, so that the value is concrete and predictable.
11. As a Beadsmith user, I want valid whole-number values from 8px through 32px, so that I have a useful accessibility range without accidentally making the interface unusable.
12. As a Beadsmith user, I want the default Markdown font size to remain 14px, so that existing users see no visual change until they choose one.
13. As a Beadsmith user, I want the live preview to update as soon as I enter a valid value, so that I can judge the result before returning to an Issue.
14. As a Beadsmith user, I want the preview to contain representative prose, headings, lists, links, inline code, and a code block, so that I can judge the complete Markdown hierarchy.
15. As a Beadsmith user, I want an empty or invalid value to show an inline validation message, so that I understand why the setting was not applied.
16. As a Beadsmith user, I want invalid input not to overwrite my last valid preference, so that a partial edit cannot corrupt the reading experience.
17. As a Beadsmith user, I want a Reset action, so that I can return to the 14px default without remembering it.
18. As a Beadsmith user, I want the Reset action to update the input and preview immediately, so that its effect is obvious.
19. As a Beadsmith user, I want a valid font-size change to be saved automatically, so that I do not need a separate Save button.
20. As a Beadsmith user, I want my Markdown font size to persist after restarting Beadsmith, so that the app continues to match my reading preference.
21. As a Beadsmith user, I want the same Markdown font size in every Workspace, so that typography behaves as an app preference rather than Workspace data.
22. As a Beadsmith user, I want Issue descriptions and comments to use the same setting, so that all authored Markdown is visually consistent.
23. As a Beadsmith user, I want Markdown headings to scale with prose, so that heading hierarchy remains proportional at every chosen size.
24. As a Beadsmith user, I want inline and block code to scale with prose, so that technical content remains legible without becoming visually disconnected.
25. As a Beadsmith user, I want lists, tables, and blockquotes to scale with prose, so that changing the base size affects the whole rendered document.
26. As a Beadsmith user, I want non-Markdown interface chrome to keep its existing size, so that this preference changes reading content rather than the entire application density.
27. As a Beadsmith user, I want Beadsmith to fall back to 14px if saved settings cannot be read, so that the Issue Explorer remains usable.
28. As a Beadsmith user, I want a settings read or save problem to be visible on the Settings page, so that persistence failures are not silently presented as successful.
29. As a keyboard user, I want the Settings button, numeric input, Reset action, and return navigation to work through normal keyboard interaction, so that the feature is accessible without a pointer.
30. As a screen-reader user, I want the font-size input, units, valid range, preview, validation, and save failure to have clear accessible names and relationships, so that the page is understandable non-visually.
31. As a future Beadsmith user, I want the settings model to be versioned and extensible, so that prose and monospace font-family preferences can be added later without replacing this feature.

## Implementation Decisions

- Model Markdown font size as an app-wide display preference, not Workspace data and not Beadwork Issue data.
- Persist settings through the existing supported Tauri store plugin in app data. Do not introduce browser local storage or direct raw Tauri invocation as a parallel persistence pattern.
- Expose settings through the project-standard typed TauRPC boundary. The frontend loads the current settings and sends valid updates through generated contracts.
- Use a separate versioned settings document rather than adding display preferences to the Workspace Catalog document. The initial document contains a schema version and a Markdown base font size.
- The supported Markdown base font-size range is 8 through 32 whole pixels, inclusive. The product default is 14px.
- Treat missing settings as the default state. Treat malformed, unsupported, or unreadable settings as a typed read failure while keeping the app usable with the default.
- Validate the range at both the frontend interaction boundary and the backend persistence boundary. The backend remains authoritative so invalid callers cannot persist unsupported values.
- Save every valid changed value automatically. Update the input and preview optimistically; if persistence fails, keep the page usable, display a save error, and retain the last successfully persisted value for the next restart.
- Keep an editable input draft separate from the last valid setting. Empty, fractional, non-numeric, and out-of-range drafts show validation and do not update the preview or call persistence.
- Add an app-level content mode for Issue Explorer versus Settings instead of introducing a routing dependency for this first utility page.
- Place Settings at the bottom of the left sidebar above the Workspace selector. It is not an Issue List View and does not display an Issue count.
- Preserve the mounted Issue Explorer state while Settings is visible so active view, search text, list scroll, and selected Issue remain available when the user returns.
- Render a focused Settings page with a page heading, a Markdown typography section, a labeled numeric input with a visible `px` unit, supporting range/default text, Reset, status/error feedback, and a representative live Markdown preview.
- Pass the resolved Markdown base size through the app's React composition to the shared Markdown renderer rather than reading persistence independently from each rendered document.
- Make the shared Markdown renderer own proportional typography. Use one base size at the Markdown article boundary and relative units for headings, prose, lists, blockquotes, tables, inline code, and code blocks.
- Apply the setting to every use of the shared Markdown renderer, including Issue descriptions, comments, and the Settings preview. Do not change Issue titles, metadata, sidebar chrome, search, or other non-Markdown text.
- Preserve the current safe Markdown behavior: raw HTML remains skipped, external HTTP links use the supported opener, and inert links remain inert.
- Keep the settings schema ready for later prose and monospace font-family fields, but do not implement font selection now.
- No glossary update or ADR is required: this is a reversible app preference built on already accepted Tauri, TauRPC, and local-store architecture.

## Testing Decisions

- Prefer external behavior assertions over implementation-detail assertions. Tests should describe what the user or typed boundary observes, not private helper names or internal component structure.
- Use the pure settings service/store seam for backend tests. A fake store should prove default loading, valid loading, malformed/unsupported data, range rejection, successful save, save failure, and reset/default behavior without launching Tauri.
- Use the typed RPC seam for contract tests. Prove that load and update operations expose the versioned settings shape, preserve typed failures, reject unsupported values, and regenerate discoverable TypeScript bindings.
- Use the app component seam for most frontend behavior. Mock the typed RPC proxy and prove sidebar navigation, initial settings loading, direct numeric entry, live preview updates, invalid-draft behavior, automatic saves, save failures, Reset, and returning to preserved Issue Explorer state.
- Use the shared Markdown renderer seam only for renderer-specific behavior. Prove that the chosen base size reaches the article boundary, descendants use proportional typography, and existing safe Markdown/link behavior does not regress.
- Follow existing React Testing Library and Vitest patterns for user-visible roles, labels, text, and interaction.
- Follow existing pure Rust fake-store patterns used by durable Workspace Catalog tests instead of requiring the real filesystem for unit coverage.
- Add one real desktop WebDriver acceptance path at the highest seam: open Settings, enter a valid size, observe the live preview and an Issue description change, restart the app against an isolated settings store, and prove the value and rendered size restore.
- Keep desktop acceptance isolated from developer preferences through an explicit test settings-store path, matching the existing isolated Workspace Catalog strategy.
- Verification for implementation slices should include focused frontend tests, focused Rust tests, generated binding verification, project lint/format checks, React Doctor regression scan, frontend build, Rust tests, and the Settings desktop acceptance path.

## Out of Scope

- Choosing the prose font family.
- Choosing the monospace font family.
- Separate prose and code font-size controls.
- Font weight, line-height, letter-spacing, color, theme, or general UI scaling preferences.
- Per-Workspace typography settings.
- Synchronizing settings between devices or storing settings in Beadwork.
- Importing or exporting settings.
- A general-purpose router or multi-page navigation framework.
- Editing Markdown or changing Markdown parsing, sanitization, link handling, or supported syntax.
- Applying the preference to non-Markdown Issue titles, metadata, navigation, search, or Workspace controls.
- Supporting fractional pixel values or values outside 8–32px.

## Further Notes

- The current Markdown prose base is 14px, code is smaller, and headings are larger. The implementation should preserve these relationships by converting fixed descendant sizes to a proportional hierarchy.
- The existing app already registers the Tauri store plugin, uses a backend-owned app-data store for durable local state, exports TauRPC TypeScript bindings, and renders descriptions/comments through one shared Markdown component. These are the primary territory constraints behind the plan.
- The Settings page is intentionally small, but its layout should leave room for later prose and monospace font-family controls in the same Markdown typography section.
- Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## Delivery Slices

1. Build the versioned settings persistence service and typed RPC contract.
2. Make shared Markdown typography accept and proportionally apply a base size.
3. Add Settings navigation, direct numeric input, validation, live preview, automatic persistence, Reset, and Issue Explorer integration.
4. Prove the complete Settings path with isolated real-desktop acceptance coverage and document the verification command.

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Settings persistence and RPC] --> C[Settings page integration]
    B[Configurable Markdown typography] --> C
    C --> D[Real desktop acceptance proof]
```
title: Add app-wide Markdown typography settings
